title: Jayson Tatum, Derrick White sound off on ‘ridiculous’ Jaylen Brown ejection
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Jaylen Brown was ejected before halftime in the Celtics' loss to the Spurs after arguing with officials.
Jayson Tatum and Derrick White criticized the ejection, agreeing the second technical was excessive for a national TV game.
Coach Joe Mazzulla and teammates supported Brown, who has previously expressed frustration over officiating this season.

title: He’s running Boston to honor of his mother’s adventurous spirit
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: James Hebert, 62, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor his late mother, Janice.
Janice Hebert enjoyed adventurous activities like hang-gliding and skydiving, inspiring her son's marathon run.
James will wear a lilac-colored shirt in tribute to his mother’s love for lilacs during the race.

title: Authorities look into possible vandalism of expensive geotubes on Nantucket
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Authorities investigate suspected vandalism of geotubes at Sconset Bluff, Nantucket, after finding intentional cuts on Feb. 2.
A $10,000 reward is offered for information leading to an arrest, with Ed Davis leading the investigation.
Winter storm damage has also impacted the geotubes, risking the bluff's erosion control system and nearby landmarks.

title: ICE detains Marlborough 14-year-old, apparently as ‘hostage’ to target her father, Rep. Trahan says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Rep. Lori Trahan says ICE detained a 14-year-old girl from Marlborough to coerce her father into deportation.
Trahan condemns the tactic, urging for the girl's immediate release and return to her family and school.
This incident mirrors a past case where ICE allegedly used a 5-year-old in Minnesota as bait for family detainment.

title: Mass. woman among victims in Utah triple homicide; fundraiser launched for husband
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Natalie Graves, an Easthampton resident, was killed in a Utah triple homicide while hiking with her aunt, Linda Dewey.
The suspect allegedly killed another woman earlier that day before stealing her vehicle and fleeing to the hiking trail.
A GoFundMe for Graves' husband, Taylor, has raised over $38,000 to help manage expenses related to her death.

title: Here’s what to know about the new $1 billion Gillette headquarters planned for Fort Point
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Procter & Gamble plans a $1 billion investment for a new Gillette headquarters in South Boston, maintaining local operations.
The new headquarters will be located at 232 A St. and incorporate a Grooming Headquarters and Technical Innovation Center.
Manufacturing operations will shift from South Boston to a new 200,000-square-foot facility in Andover, beginning this year.

title: 4 things to know about new Patriots edge rusher Dre’Mont Jones
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Patriots signed Dre'Mont Jones to boost their pass rush after ranking 26th in sacks in 2025.
Jones had 15 quarterback hits in nine games with the Ravens last season, the most on the team.
Jones is a 6-foot-3, 281-pound versatile player who can play both outside linebacker and defensive tackle.

title: Seamark at Encore Boston to close, less than a month after workers unionize
author: Katelyn Umholtz
bullets: Seamark Seafood & Cocktails at Encore Boston Harbor will permanently close on March 29 due to 'economic challenges.'
The closure comes less than a month after 56 workers voted to unionize, raising claims of retaliatory action by UNITE HERE Local 26.
Seamark's workers had yet to begin contract negotiations; the union accused Seamark of aggressive anti-union tactics.

title: A nearly 100-year-old Catholic school in Medford is closing
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: St. Joseph School in Medford, serving pre-K to eighth grade, will close due to declining enrollment and financial pressures.
Enrollment dropped from 222 students in 2021-2022 to 169, with families unable to bear rising educational and maintenance costs.
Families will receive tuition assistance options, while faculty and staff are prioritized for new positions in other Catholic schools.

title: This 14-time marathoner is running the Boston Marathon for his daughter and children’s health
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Chris Ruggio, 57, from Duxbury is running the 2026 Boston Marathon for Boston Children's Hospital charity team.
Ruggio's daughter, Holly, has juvenile idiopathic arthritis and is undergoing care at Boston Children’s Hospital.
To date, Ruggio has raised over $70,000 for children's health charities through his marathon participation.
